potential_missing.source_sentence: In order to make it efficient and improve the current system of PDS, we are implementing e-Ration Shop.
The main drawback in the current system is that the PDS has been criticized for its urban bias and its failure to serve the poorer sections of the population effectively.
Also, many retail shopkeepers have a large number of bogus cards to sell food grains in the open market.
What’s meant for them or the farm produce procured by the PDS is diverted to the open market.
So in order to avoid all these drawbacks, we are going to use the e-Ration Shop which will help us to avoid the corruption in PDS if not eradicate it.

The various importance of the proposed case study is every shop’s geo-location will be located on the map, all the information regarding shop and shopkeeper respective to the ration card and region, shop opening and closing details will be available, stock provided based on category, retailers can add, edit, delete and update their shop’s other items also, retailers can advertise their shops, stock availability will be notified on regular basis, product delivery feature, complaint forum for complaints by users, easy payment system by PayTM, COD etc., admin and moderator features for government and shopkeepers and authentication system by Aadhaar number and One-Time Pin (OTP) .

potential_hallucinations.requirement: The system shall display the geo-location of shops on a map for easy identification.
The system shall provide detailed information about shops, shopkeepers, ration cards, and regions.
The system shall display the opening and closing hours of shops for users' reference.
The system shall enable retailers to manage their shop's items by adding, editing, deleting, and updating them.
The system shall allow retailers to advertise their shops for increased visibility.
The system shall notify users about the availability of stocks on a regular basis.
The system shall implement a product delivery feature for convenient customer experience.
The system shall provide a complaint forum where users can raise their concerns and issues.
The system shall integrate an easy payment system using PayTM, Cash on Delivery (COD), and other convenient options.
The system shall implement administrative and moderation features for the government and shopkeepers to manage their accounts effectively.
The system shall implement a secure authentication system using Aadhaar numbers and One-Time Pins (OTPs) for user verification.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: The traditional PDS is used to distribute grocery items to India’s poor who are valid ration cardholders.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall verify the authenticity of ration cards before allowing users to place orders.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: Many retail shopkeepers have a large number of bogus cards to sell food grains in the open market.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall prevent the sale of food grains using fake or duplicate ration cards.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: Most of the time, users do not get their rightful entitlement in terms of quantity.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall ensure that users receive their allocated quantity of grocery items as per the norms of PDS.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall make the distribution process fully automated and computerized.
hallucinations.reason: This requirement is not mentioned in the source text. The original text only mentions making the current system efficient and improving it, but does not specify automation or computerization.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall remove the drawbacks associated with issuing products based on ration cards.
hallucinations.reason: This requirement is too vague and does not accurately reflect the source text. The original text mentions urban bias and failure to serve poorer sections, but does not specify removing drawbacks.

duplicates.requirement_a: The system shall allow ration cardholders to place orders for grocery items online.
duplicates.requirement_b: The system shall enable users to order their grocery items online using the e-Ration Shop website.
duplicates.reason: These two requirements are similar and can be combined into a single requirement.

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall display the geo-location of shops on a map for easy identification.
ambiguities.reason: This requirement is ambiguous because it does not specify how the geo-location will be displayed or what information will be included.

Source text:
<<<
Public Distribution System (PDS) is an Indian food security system. It is established by the
Government of India under the Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food, and Public Distribution and
managed jointly with state governments in India. The traditional PDS is used to distribute grocery
items to India’s poor who are valid ration cardholders. The validity and the allocation of the ration
cards are monitored by the state governments. A ration cardholder should be given 35 kg of food
grain as per the norms of PDS. However, there are concerns about the efficiency of the distribution
process. In order to make it efficient and improve the current system of PDS, we are implementing
e-Ration Shop. Here, we are going to make a website for shopping purposes. Using this website
ration cardholder can order his/her grocery items from the PDS online. The main reason for using
this website is to make this process computerized and to remove the drawbacks of the present way
of issuing products based on the ration card. The main drawback in the current system is that
the PDS has been criticized for its urban bias and its failure to serve the poorer sections of the
population effectively. Also, many retail shopkeepers have a large number of bogus cards to sell
food grains in the open market. Many PDS dealers resort to malpractice since they acquire less
Salary so, by this project we are solving this problem too. Most of the time, users do not get their
rightful entitlement in terms of quantity. What’s meant for them or the farm produce procured by
the PDS is diverted to the open market. So in order to avoid all these drawbacks, we are going to
use the e-Ration Shop which will help us to avoid the corruption in PDS if not eradicate it. The
various importance of the proposed case study is every shop’s geo-location will be located on the
map, all the information regarding shop and shopkeeper respective to the ration card and region,
shop opening and closing details will be available, stock provided based on category, retailers can
add, edit, delete and update their shop’s other items also, retailers can advertise their shops, stock
availability will be notified on regular basis, product delivery feature, complaint forum for complaints by users, easy payment system by PayTM, COD etc., admin and moderator features for
government and shopkeepers and authentication system by Aadhaar number and One-Time Pin
(OTP) (Figures 15.1–15.8).
>>>
